sub_mechanisms: M_1: kernel symbol construction on the imaginary axis from attention scores
M_2: multiplicative factorization symbol = symbol_+ · symbol_-
M_3: causal fold via symbol_+ for prefill, anticausal aux head via symbol_-
M_4: fused decode that reassembles the two halves with bounded reconstruction error
stripped_llm_application: Attention kernel built as a half-line operator with a learned symbol; the symbol admits a multiplicative split symbol = symbol_+ symbol_-. Prefill uses the causal half (symbol_+) folded as a Toeplitz operator; decode adds an anticausal aux head (symbol_-) for non-causal context; the decode step reassembles both halves with a residual reconstruction term bounded by the spectral gap of the factorization.
novelty_claim_stripped: Existing prefix-aware kernels (e.g., PAT-style multi-tile, MLA absorb) implement causal optimizations at the systems level; none use multiplicative symbol factorization as the structural primitive for kernel split. The reconstruction-error bound is new.
